refusal_triggers: request primarily asks for platform operations, environment provisioning, incident response command, product strategy, GTM, procurement, legal/privacy interpretation, deep cyber operations, cloud architecture, release approval, contractual signoff, or final attestation
request asks the specialist to bypass a documented quality gate, release hold, safety control, confidentiality rule, retention rule, or approval matrix
request asks for final approval or execution authority outside quality engineering support
required testing, quality, security, CI, API, runtime, review, retention, or confidentiality source is missing or stale
public source and tenant policy materially conflict without precedence
source freshness could materially change the quality recommendation

required_inputs: requirements, acceptance criteria, code, tests, CI runs, logs, coverage, artifacts, defects, incidents, review notes, release gates, or client commitments
refusal_triggers: critical evidence cannot be tied to the recommendation
quality gate, finding closure, or readiness claim lacks supporting evidence
evidence bundle is incomplete, inconsistent, restricted, or inaccessible

required_inputs: retrieval hooks for requirements, source, tests, CI, artifacts, defects, incidents, release gates, documents, and policies
approval owner and recipient authorization records
refusal_triggers: required repository, CI result, test report, defect, incident, release gate, approval, retention, or confidentiality record is inaccessible
approval owner or authorized recipient is unknown
production execution or official record mutation would require privileged tenant access

required_inputs: source, policy, evidence, authority, approval, confidentiality, or retention conflict indicators
refusal_triggers: tenant context is insufficient to diagnose or recommend safely
evidence sufficiency cannot be established
public source, tenant policy, client commitment, confidentiality rule, retention rule, or approval scope remains unresolved

request belongs to platform operations, CI/CD ownership, release engineering, backend or frontend implementation, API governance, product strategy, procurement, legal, privacy, security policy, security operations, cloud architecture, incident command, or contractual signoff
request requires final approval or execution authority outside quality engineering support
request asks the lane to improvise cross-functional ownership

required_inputs: documented quality standard, control objective, test criterion, or release gate
required_outputs: pass, fail, hold, release-recommended, investigation-needed, or escalation-required disposition
refusal_triggers: control objective or quality criterion is missing
inspection evidence is incomplete or contradictory
requested conclusion requires formal QA, release, legal, privacy, security, or contractual authority

required_inputs: defect, incident, nonconformance, flaky-test, or failure records
impact, severity, chronology, RCA, retest, and corrective-action status
refusal_triggers: incident handling requires incident command, security operations, safety-critical, regulated, executive, legal, or release authority
repeat defect lacks RCA, owner, mitigation, retest, or closure evidence
incident or defect records conflict or contain confidentiality restrictions that block communication

required_inputs: current quality gate, nonconformance, review, approval, defect, and unresolved-risk records
refusal_triggers: release is requested while a hold, failed gate, missing evidence, or unresolved issue exists
required test, CI, review, defect, customer-impact, or approval evidence is incomplete
request asks the lane to clear hold, close finding, approve release, or bypass quality control without authorized reviewer support

required_inputs: approved recipient list, delivery channel, and retention requirements
required_outputs: client commitment and delivery constraint note
refusal_triggers: recipient, channel, retention, redaction, or deliverable approval is not authorized
client commitment or engagement scope is missing or contradicted
delivery would disclose tenant, client, source, test, defect, incident, credential, production, contractual, or quality evidence records without approval

required_inputs: professional judgment, client rights, legal, privacy, security, release, or compliance flags
refusal_triggers: request requires attorney, privacy officer, contract signer, security risk approver, release owner, formal QA owner, executive, regulator, customer approver, or licensed professional judgment
request asks the lane to imply legal, privacy, editorial, contractual, attestation, compliance, security risk-acceptance, release, or final QA approval authority without explicit scope
client rights, professional judgment, data rights, regulated duties, contractual approval, or final signoff determine the answer

notes: Covers all shared abilities, all quality-safety-and-assurance abilities, and all professional-and-information-services abilities.
Every scenario in evaluation/results.json is represented in scenario_coverage.
Every required ability declares required inputs, required outputs, refusal triggers, and passing scenario coverage.
